## Architecture

`agent-init` is a Go CLI that scaffolds repositories for agentic development. Entry point is `cmd/agent-init/main.go`, which wires stdio and build metadata into `internal/cli`. The CLI dispatches five subcommands: `init`, `add-tracker`, `list-flavors`, `list-trackers`, and `version`. For migration convenience, calling the binary without a subcommand (or with flags or a target path first) is treated as `init`.

The codebase has three subsystems:

- **`internal/cli/`** owns argument parsing and user-facing command behavior. It resolves a requested flavor from `internal/flavors`, an optional tracker from `internal/trackers`, and hands a concrete request to `internal/scaffold`.
- **`internal/flavors/`** defines `Flavor` (the metadata that drives scaffolding) and a registry of the shipped flavors. Each flavor is its own subpackage with an `embed.FS` template tree.
- **`internal/scaffold/`** is the engine that walks a flavor's templates, layers the optional common overlay, renders `.tmpl` files with `text/template`, copies everything else verbatim, sets executable bits on declared paths, creates declared symlinks, and (unless suppressed) runs `git init`.

A separate **`internal/trackers/`** subsystem powers `add-tracker`. Each tracker (`gh`, `jira`, `ado`) is a subpackage with its own template tree and an `MCPServer()` map; `mcp.go` merges that map into the target project's `.mcp.json` idempotently.

### Flavors

Five flavors live in the default registry, all wired in `internal/flavors/registry.go`:

| Name | Kind | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `fullstack` | code | TypeScript/Node, Playwright recording, OpenAPI client hooks. |
| `go-cli` | code | Go CLI scaffold; `cmd/{{.ProjectName}}/`, `internal/`, cross-build, golangci-lint. |
| `go-backend` | code | Go HTTP backend; `cmd/server`, `internal/api`, `/healthz`. |
| `claude-cowork` | doc-collab | OneDrive-friendly: no devcontainer, no Justfile, no symlinks; provides its own `NextSteps`. |
| `project-management` | doc-collab | Epics/decisions/stakeholders workspace; ships five skills; extendable via `add-tracker`. |

Every `Flavor` (defined in `internal/flavors/flavor.go`) carries `Name`, `DisplayName`, `Description`, `Templates`, `TemplateRoot`, optional `CommonTemplates`/`CommonRoot`, `ExecutablePaths`, `Symlinks`, and an optional `NextSteps func(target string) string` for the post-scaffold message. Code flavors set `Symlinks` via `codeFlavorSymlinks()` (the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md trio). Doc-collab flavors leave `Symlinks` nil and override `NextSteps`.

### Common overlay

`internal/flavors/common/templates/` holds files every code flavor needs (currently the `.agent/scripts/{check,gen-codemap,review}.sh` trio). The scaffold engine walks the flavor first, then walks common, skipping any relative path the flavor already claimed. Doc-collab flavors omit `CommonTemplates` entirely.

### This repo vs. shipped templates

There are two kinds of files in this repo and confusing them is the most common mistake:

- **This repo's own working files**: top-level `.agent/`, `.devcontainer/`, `Justfile`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.github/workflows/`. These exist to develop `agent-init` itself.
- **Shipped templates**: everything under `internal/flavors/*/templates/`. These are embedded into the binary and written into downstream projects.

When tempted to "fix" this repo's top-level scaffolding, first ask whether the change actually belongs in a flavor template.

### CI

`.github/workflows/pr.yml` runs the full done-gate (`just check`) plus pre-commit hygiene on every PR. `.github/workflows/release.yml` runs the same gate on push to `main`, builds Linux amd64/arm64 and macOS arm64 binaries via a matrix, generates SHA-256 checksums, and publishes a GitHub Release.

## Key files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `cmd/agent-init/main.go` | CLI entry point; injects build metadata into `internal/cli`. |
| `internal/cli/cli.go` | All five subcommand handlers and flag parsing. |
| `internal/cli/cli_test.go` | CLI behavior tests including `add-tracker` idempotency and multi-tracker coexistence. |
| `internal/flavors/flavor.go` | `Flavor` and `Symlink` structs — the shape every flavor implements. |
| `internal/flavors/registry.go` | Default flavor registry; `codeFlavorSymlinks()`; lookup and listing helpers. |
| `internal/flavors/registry_test.go` | Ensures every flavor's templates parse via `text/template`. |
| `internal/flavors/common/flavor.go` | Common-overlay embed + executable paths (the shared `.agent/scripts/`). |
| `internal/flavors/common/templates/.agent/scripts/` | Shipped done-gate (`check.sh`), codemap (`gen-codemap.sh`), reviewer (`review.sh`). |
| `internal/flavors/fullstack/` | Fullstack flavor: embed point, executable paths, template tree. |
| `internal/flavors/gocli/` | Go CLI flavor; note the `cmd/{{.ProjectName}}/main.go.tmpl` path-templating gotcha (see Conventions). |
| `internal/flavors/gobackend/` | Go HTTP backend flavor; templated tests at `internal/api/handlers_test.go`. |
| `internal/flavors/claudecowork/` | Doc-collab flavor; overrides `NextSteps`, leaves `Symlinks` nil. |
| `internal/flavors/projectmgmt/` | Doc-collab flavor; ships five skills and integrates with `add-tracker`. |
| `internal/scaffold/scaffold.go` | Scaffold engine: walk, render, skip/force, symlinks, executable bits, optional `git init`. |
| `internal/scaffold/scaffold_test.go` | Engine guarantees: substitution, skip-existing, force, dry-run, symlinks, common overlay, path-templating. |
| `internal/trackers/registry.go` | Default tracker registry (`gh`, `jira`, `ado`). |
| `internal/trackers/tracker.go` | `Tracker` struct; mirrors `Flavor` for the tracker subsystem. |
| `internal/trackers/mcp.go` | Idempotent merge of a tracker's MCP server entry into `.mcp.json`. |
| `internal/trackers/{ado,gh,jira}/` | Tracker subpackages; each embeds its templates and exposes `MCPServer()`. |
| `internal/testflags/testflags.go` | The `-update` flag shared by golden tests for regenerating snapshots. |
| `test/golden_test.go` | Golden snapshot tests; scaffolds each flavor and diffs against `testdata/golden/<flavor>/`. |
| `testdata/golden/<flavor>/` | Per-flavor golden snapshots; regenerate with `just smoke-test-update`. |
| `docs/` | Per-feature documentation (`cli.md`, `engine/flavor-hooks.md`, `flavors/<name>.md`); backlog in `docs/README.md`. |
| `.claude/skills/add-flavor/SKILL.md` | Walks the flavor-authoring checklist end-to-end. |
| `.claude/skills/feature-doc/SKILL.md` | Adds or refreshes a `docs/` entry and drains the backlog. |
| `Justfile` | Local command surface: `check`, `fmt`, `lint`, `lint-shell`, `mod-tidy`, `typecheck`, `test`, `cross-build`, `smoke-test`, `smoke-test-update`, `vulncheck`. |
| `.agent/scripts/check.sh` | This repo's done-gate (Go-specific; harder gates than the shipped `common` check.sh). |
| `.agent/scripts/gen-codemap.sh` | Regenerates the auto-generated section above. |
| `.agent/scripts/review.sh` | Invokes the reviewer agent against the working diff. |
| `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` | Local devcontainer: Go, goimports, golangci-lint, govulncheck, Node (agent CLIs only), gh, just, pre-commit. |
| `.github/workflows/pr.yml` | PR CI: pre-commit hygiene + `just check`. |
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Main-branch CI: gate, matrix build (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64), GitHub release. |
| `.pre-commit-config.yaml` | Local pre-commit hooks; pre-push stage runs the full done-gate. |
| `go.mod` | Module + toolchain pin. Currently `go 1.26` / `toolchain go1.26.3`; no external dependencies. |
| `README.md` | User-facing build/usage docs. |

## Where to add things

- **CLI command or flag behavior** → edit `internal/cli/cli.go`, add tests in `internal/cli/cli_test.go`. Update `docs/cli.md`.
- **Scaffold engine behavior** → edit `internal/scaffold/scaffold.go`, add tests in `internal/scaffold/scaffold_test.go`. Consider whether the change deserves a `docs/engine/<topic>.md` entry.
- **Existing flavor template change** → edit `internal/flavors/<name>/templates/`, then `just smoke-test`. If intentional, regenerate goldens with `just smoke-test-update` and review the diff carefully.
- **New flavor** → use the `/add-flavor` skill, or follow the checklist in CLAUDE.md ("Flavor authoring"). Don't skip the golden snapshot or the docs entry under `docs/flavors/`.
- **New tracker** → add `internal/trackers/<name>/` (templates + `MCPServer()`), register in `internal/trackers/registry.go`, add CLI tests. Update the `add-tracker` docs entry.
- **Common-overlay change (affects every code flavor)** → edit `internal/flavors/common/templates/`. Never copy a common file into a flavor "just to be safe" — overrides should be deliberate.
- **New skill** → add `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`. Reserve skills for workflows that are multi-step, error-prone, and likely to recur.
- **New per-feature doc** → use the `/feature-doc` skill or follow the convention in `docs/README.md`.
- **This repo's CI** → edit `.github/workflows/`. Keep workflow Go versions read from `go.mod` so a single bump propagates.
- **This repo's local devcontainer** → edit `.devcontainer/`. Not a shipped template — don't propagate flavor-specific tools (Playwright, openapi generators) into it.
- **Future global gitignore support** → add under `internal/gitconfig/` with fake-`HOME` tests. No silent mutation of the user's git config outside the documented `--gitignore-global` flag path.

## Conventions specific to this codebase

- **Templating is two-layered.** Path templating applies to every file (so `cmd/{{.ProjectName}}/main.go.tmpl` renders to `cmd/myproject/main.go`). Content templating applies only to `.tmpl` files. The `.tmpl` extension is the explicit opt-in; files without it are copied verbatim. This matters because shell scripts, GitHub Actions, and Ansible/Helm configs legitimately contain `{{ }}`.
- **`cmd/{{.ProjectName}}/` files must use `.tmpl` extensions even for `.go` files.** A plain `.go` file under that directory breaks `go build ./...` in this repo because Go tooling can't parse `{` as a package path. The `.tmpl` extension hides it from Go tooling; `text/template` parses no-op content fine.
- **`embed.FS` preserves neither executable bits nor symlinks.** Executable paths live in each flavor's `ExecutablePaths()`; symlinks live in `Flavor.Symlinks` and are created post-write by the scaffold engine.
- **Common overlay wins are flavor-side.** When a flavor and common claim the same relative path, the flavor's file wins (the engine tracks claimed paths during the flavor pass).
- **Symlink trio for code flavors:** `AGENTS.md` → `.agent/AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md` → `.agent/CLAUDE.md`, `.agent/CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md`. Doc-collab flavors skip this because sync clients (OneDrive, Dropbox) eat symlinks.
- **The done-gate must run after documentation-only edits too.** It regenerates this codemap and preserves the hand-written section.
- **The auto-generated tree omits hidden dirs** (`.agent/`, `.devcontainer/`, `.github/`, `.claude/`). When auditing the shipped surface of a flavor, use `git ls-files internal/flavors/<name>/` rather than relying on the codemap.
- **No external dependencies in `go.mod`.** Standard library first. Additions need justification in the PR.
